projects.title: Structural Floor Reinforcement
projects.subtitle: Northeast corner of the nave
projects.short_desc: A sagging section of the church floor must be reinforced immediately to ensure the safety of parishioners and prevent further structural damage.
projects.body:
We have discovered that a sagging section of the church floor must be reinforced without delay. This is in the Northeast corner of the church, above the accounting room and the stage where the children meet for Bible study and play.

A sag among some of the pews was noticed, but the severity of the sag in the hall ceiling below is what caused us to call an engineer. The sag has been progressing for many decades but has reached a point where it cannot be allowed to continue without constituting a negligence for safety and risking possible damage to water pipes along the hall ceiling below.

The funds gathered will allow us to install long-term reinforcement to the area while taking fewer of the needed funds out of parish investments. The engineer also examined the rest of the floor and saw nothing else of concern.
projects.quote: The temple of God is holy, and you are that temple.

updates.title: Engineer Assessment Complete
updates.body: A structural engineer examined the floor and confirmed the sag requires immediate reinforcement. The rest of the floor was examined and found to be sound.

projects.title: Hall Painting & Wall Repair
projects.subtitle: Restoring the parish hall
projects.short_desc: The church hall, where we gather for fellowship and classes after Liturgy, has numerous areas of peeling paint and wall damage remaining from a prior flood.
projects.body:
The church hall is where parish life happens between Sundays. It is where we gather after Liturgy, where the children have their classes, where newcomers first sit down and have coffee with a parishioner. It is, in the best sense, a working room.

Decades of use, along with damage remaining from a prior flood along the lower walls, have left the hall with peeling paint and areas of deteriorating wall surface. This project will restore the hall to a condition worthy of the hospitality we offer to all who come through our doors.

Volunteers with painting experience are especially welcome on this project. Your time goes as far as your donation.
projects.volunteer_desc: Volunteers with painting or drywall experience are welcome. Contact the parish office to get involved.

updates.title: Project Opened
updates.body: The hall painting and wall repair project has been opened for donations and volunteer sign-ups. A full assessment of the scope of work is underway.

projects.title: Outward Facing Fund
projects.subtitle: Community, mission, and mercy work
projects.short_desc: An ongoing fund supporting the parish's outward-facing work: feeding the hungry, supporting families in need, and serving our neighbors in Jersey City.
projects.body:
Orthodox Christianity has always understood that the Church does not exist for itself. The Liturgy sends us outward. The mercy we receive, we are called to extend to our neighbors.

This fund supports the parish's ongoing work in the community: food distributions, support for families facing hardship, and the small acts of presence and care that constitute mission in a neighborhood. It is not a single project with a finish line. It is the ongoing expression of who we are trying to be as a parish.

Gifts to this fund are used at the discretion of Fr. Solomon and the parish council, directed toward the most immediate needs as they arise throughout the year.
projects.quote: For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was a stranger and you welcomed me.

projects.title: Paschal Vestment Set
projects.subtitle: In memory of Matushka Anna Petrov
projects.short_desc: A new set of white Paschal vestments for Fr. Solomon, commissioned in memory of a beloved parishioner who gave thirty years of her life to this parish.
projects.body:
Matushka Anna Petrov served Saints Peter and Paul for three decades. She sewed, organized, cooked, and prayed. She arrived early and stayed late. She knew every child in the parish by name and remembered every birthday. She fell asleep in the Lord in October 2023.

Her family has asked that gifts in her memory be directed toward something that will endure in the life of the parish. A full set of white Paschal vestments will be seen and worn at every Pascha for many years to come. It is the kind of gift that speaks across time.

The set includes the priestly riassa, epitrachelion, phelonion, and cuffs, all in white with gold embroidery. The work will be done by an Orthodox vestment maker.
projects.give_label: Give in Memory of Matushka Anna
projects.memorial_for: In memory of Matushka Anna Petrov, who gave thirty years of her life to this parish and fell asleep in the Lord in October 2023.
projects.quote: Well done, good and faithful servant.

updates.title: Vestment Maker Selected
updates.body: An Orthodox vestment atelier has been selected. Design review is underway with the Petrov family.

updates.title: Campaign Opened
updates.body: The memorial vestment campaign was opened at the request of the Petrov family. All gifts go toward the full Paschal set.

projects.title: Thanksgiving Food Drive 2025
projects.subtitle: Feeding our neighbors this November
projects.short_desc: Each year the parish collects non-perishable food and funds to assemble Thanksgiving boxes for families in our neighborhood. Last year we served 47 families.
projects.body:
Every November, the parish gathers food and funds to put together Thanksgiving boxes for families in the Jersey City neighborhood who could use the help. It is one of the most concrete things we do as a parish community, and the response year after year has been generous.

Last year, 47 families received a box. This year we are aiming for 60. The boxes include a full Thanksgiving meal: a turkey, stuffing, canned vegetables, cranberry sauce, and a pie. Each box costs approximately $35 to assemble.

You can give online, drop non-perishables in the box in the narthex, or sign up to help sort and assemble boxes the week before Thanksgiving. Every form of participation counts.
projects.volunteer_desc: Volunteers are needed the weekend of November 22-23 to sort donations and assemble boxes. Contact the parish office to sign up.
projects.quote: For I was hungry and you gave me food.

updates.title: Drive Opened
updates.body: The 2025 food drive is open. Non-perishables can be dropped in the narthex collection box beginning now through November 19.

projects.title: Parish Library Expansion
projects.subtitle: Books for formation at every stage of life
projects.short_desc: The parish library is being expanded with Orthodox children's books, Lives of Saints, patristic texts, and catechetical resources for inquirers and new members.
projects.body:
One of the quietest and most lasting forms of formation happens through books. A well-stocked parish library is one of the gifts a parish can give its members across generations.

We are building out our library with books at every level: illustrated Lives of Saints for children, introductory texts for inquirers, solid patristic reading for those going deeper, and reference materials for the clergy. The goal is a collection that a family can return to at every stage of their life in the Church.

There is no fixed dollar goal for this project. Every gift, however small, adds something permanent to the parish's resources. If you have Orthodox books in good condition you would like to donate, contact the parish office.
projects.volunteer_desc: Volunteers are needed to catalog new acquisitions and keep the lending shelf organized. No experience required.
projects.quote: The reading of Holy Scripture is a great bulwark against sin.

updates.title: First Batch of Books Acquired
updates.body: Twenty-two new titles have been added to the lending shelf, including the full set of Little Sprouts Lives of Saints for children and several patristic introductions.

updates.title: Project Opened
updates.body: The library expansion project was opened for ongoing donations and book donations.

projects.title: Parish Kitchen Renovation
projects.subtitle: Serving hundreds every Sunday
projects.short_desc: The parish kitchen serves coffee hour, memorial luncheons, and parish dinners every week. Aging equipment and outdated fixtures need replacement to keep pace with parish life.
projects.body:
The parish kitchen is one of the most-used rooms in the building. Every Sunday after Liturgy, coffee hour is set up and served. Several times a month it hosts a memorial luncheon or a parish dinner. The children's summer program uses it daily. It is not a back room. It is a room where the parish is fed.

The kitchen's equipment dates from several decades ago. The commercial range has been repaired more than it should be. The countertop has split. The ventilation hood does not meet current code. This renovation will replace the range, install a proper stainless steel work surface, upgrade the hood, and repaint.

It is not a glamorous project. But it is one of the most practical gifts you can give to the daily life of this parish.

updates.title: Contractor Quotes Received
updates.body: Three contractor quotes have been received. The parish council is reviewing and expects to award the contract by May.

updates.title: Project Opened
updates.body: The kitchen renovation project was formally opened after an assessment identified the range, countertop, and ventilation hood as priorities.

projects.title: Choir Loft Restoration
projects.subtitle: Returning the loft to full use
projects.short_desc: The choir loft has been inoperable for a number of years. This project will replace the damaged floor, restore the railing, and make the space safe for the choir and their children.
projects.body:
Let us who mystically represent the cherubim... These are the first words of the Cherubic Hymn, sung during the Great Entrance. The choir leads the parish in the ancient words of praise.

At Saints Peter and Paul, as at many parishes, the choir once sang from the choir loft. The loft has been inoperable for a number of years, but we now have the volunteers and the will to restore it.

The damaged floor will be replaced. The balcony railing will be made safe, especially for the children of choir members who come to sing. Flooring will be installed. By prior generosity, a chandelier has already been acquired to hang above the loft.

This project is currently on hold while priority structural floor work is addressed. Funds already raised are held and will be applied when the project resumes.
projects.volunteer_desc: Volunteers with carpentry or flooring experience will be needed when the project resumes. Contact the parish office to express interest.
projects.quote: Let us who mystically represent the cherubim...

updates.title: Project Placed on Hold
updates.body: This project has been placed on hold while priority structural floor reinforcement work is addressed. Funds already raised are held and will be applied when the project resumes.

updates.title: Fundraising Opened
updates.body: The choir loft restoration project was formally opened. Volunteers began assessment of the work needed.

projects.title: Narthex Icon of the Theotokos
projects.subtitle: Commissioning a new icon for the entrance
projects.short_desc: A commissioned icon of the Theotokos is planned for the narthex, to greet all who enter the cathedral. The project is paused while structural priorities are addressed.
projects.body:
The narthex is the threshold between the world and the nave. It is where we slow down, light a candle, venerate an icon, and prepare ourselves for worship. At Saints Peter and Paul, the narthex has long lacked a dedicated icon of the Theotokos in a permanent, prominent position.

This project will commission a full-size icon of the Mother of God from an Orthodox iconographer, to be mounted in the narthex in a carved wooden frame. The icon will serve as the first greeting for every person who walks through our doors: parishioner, visitor, and inquirer alike.

The project is currently on hold while the structural floor reinforcement work is prioritized. Funds raised will be held and applied when the commission is ready to proceed.
projects.quote: It is truly meet to bless you, O Theotokos.

updates.title: Project Placed on Hold
updates.body: The icon commission has been placed on hold while structural floor work is addressed. Funds raised are held.

updates.title: Iconographer Consultation
updates.body: Fr. Solomon met with an Orthodox iconographer to discuss style, size, and composition. A design proposal is pending.

updates.title: Project Opened
updates.body: The narthex icon project was opened following a parish council discussion about the entrance space.

projects.title: Chandelier Cleaning & Restoration
projects.subtitle: Restoring the light of the nave
projects.short_desc: The chandeliers of the nave were cleaned, missing crystals replaced, and worn bulbs and sockets restored. They now shine as they have not for many years.
projects.body:
The beauty of the Orthodox temple testifies to the beauty of God and to the peace offered by the Gospel. For anyone who has been inside Saints Peter and Paul, the chandeliers feature prominently in their memories. The lights within our services remind us of the Father of Lights who gives clarity to our lives.

Our chandeliers were in need of restoration so that they could keep being the uplifting sight they have been to parishioners and visitors for many years. The whole chandelier was brightened with a cleaning. Missing crystals were replaced. Bulbs and sockets that had worn out were changed to bring the chandelier back to its former glory.
projects.completion_note: Thank you to all who helped make the chandeliers shine beautifully clear.
projects.quote: I am the light of the world.

updates.title: Project Complete
updates.body: The chandeliers have been restored to their full brilliance. Thank you to all who gave toward this project.

updates.title: Fundraising Opened
updates.body: The chandelier restoration campaign was launched.

projects.title: Choir Loft Railing
projects.subtitle: Safety railing for the choir loft
projects.short_desc: A brass railing was added to the top of the existing loft railing to increase safety for choir members and their children.
projects.body:
To increase safety, a brass railing was added to the top of the existing choir loft railing, heightening the barrier to a safe standard for choir members and the children who come to sing.

The completed railing appears original to the building and complements the historic character of the nave. Thank you to all who gave toward this work.
projects.completion_note: The goal has been reached. The rail looks great and appears original to the building.

updates.title: Project Complete
updates.body: The brass railing has been installed. It looks great and appears original to the building. Goal fully reached.

updates.title: Fundraising Opened
updates.body: The choir loft railing project was launched to increase safety in the loft.

projects.title: Ukrainian Orthodox Relief Collection
projects.subtitle: Support for Orthodox Christians in Ukraine
projects.short_desc: In spring 2022, the parish organized a special collection to support Orthodox Christians displaced by the war in Ukraine. The response from our parishioners was extraordinary.
projects.body:
When war came to Ukraine in February 2022, parishes throughout the Orthodox Church responded. Saints Peter and Paul opened a special collection to support Orthodox relief efforts through the OCA and direct channels to Ukrainian parishes in need.

Over six weeks, the parish collected funds, clothing, and medical supplies. Parishioners with Ukrainian family connections helped coordinate shipments. Fr. Solomon corresponded with clergy in affected areas to ensure the help reached where it was most needed.

The funds raised exceeded all expectations. We are grateful to every parishioner who gave, and we continue to pray for all those suffering in Ukraine and throughout the world.
projects.completion_note: The collection raised $6,200 and was distributed through OCA relief channels. Thank you to all who gave.
projects.give_label: Give to the Outward Facing Fund
projects.quote: Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.

updates.title: Collection Complete
updates.body: $6,200 was distributed through OCA relief channels. Clothing and medical supplies were coordinated separately and shipped directly.

updates.title: Collection Opened
updates.body: The parish opened a special collection for Ukrainian Orthodox relief following the outbreak of war.

projects.title: Catechesis Classroom Furnishings
projects.subtitle: Equipping the room where people enter the Faith
projects.short_desc: New chairs, tables, a whiteboard, and basic supplies for the catechesis classroom, where inquirers are received and prepared for reception into the Orthodox Church.
projects.body:
The room where catechumens meet with Fr. Solomon is a significant space. It is where people who are searching for the Church, or who have found it and want to go deeper, sit and ask their questions. It deserves to be a room that takes them seriously.

This project furnished the catechesis classroom with sturdy chairs and tables, a whiteboard, basic supplies, and a small icon corner. It is a small project in dollar terms, but it serves the parish's most important ongoing work: the formation of new members in the Faith.
projects.completion_note: The classroom is fully furnished and in use. Thank you to all who gave.
projects.quote: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations.

updates.title: Project Complete
updates.body: All furnishings have been installed. The classroom is in active use for the fall catechesis series.

updates.title: Fundraising Opened
updates.body: The catechesis classroom project was opened ahead of the fall season.

projects.title: Main Entrance Door Restoration
projects.subtitle: The first thing every visitor sees
projects.short_desc: The main entrance doors of the cathedral were stripped, refinished, and weather-sealed, restoring the historic woodwork and protecting it for decades to come.
projects.body:
The main entrance doors of Saints Peter and Paul are among the most visible features of the building from Grand Street. They are the first thing a visitor sees, and the last thing a parishioner touches on the way out after Liturgy. They are old, and they are beautiful, and they had been neglected for too long.

This project stripped the doors to bare wood, repaired areas of rot and damage, applied a period-appropriate stain, and sealed the doors against weather. The hardware was cleaned and restored. The threshold was replaced.

The doors now look as they must have when the building was dedicated. We are grateful to all who gave toward this work.
projects.completion_note: The doors have been fully restored. Thank you to the parishioners who made this possible.
projects.quote: I am the door. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved.

updates.title: Restoration Complete
updates.body: The doors have been restored and sealed. The work was completed ahead of schedule.

updates.title: Work Underway
updates.body: A restoration carpenter began stripping the doors. Rot repair took longer than expected but has been completed successfully.

updates.title: Project Opened
updates.body: Fundraising opened for the main entrance door restoration.
